message:
test(readme): integration test for NanOps section (fd90.191 / br-1r7zz)

Adds readme_nanops_compiles_and_runs covering all 23 null-skipping
primitives documented in README "NanOps" (lines 792-823):

19 scalar reductions:
- nansum, nanmean, nancount, nanmin, nanmax (basic)
- nanmedian, nanvar(ddof), nanstd(ddof), nansem(ddof) (distribution)
- nanprod, nanptp, nanskew, nankurt (extended)
- nanquantile(q), nanargmax, nanargmin (positional)
- nannunique, nanany, nanall (boolean / cardinality)

4 cumulative transforms:
- nancumsum, nancumprod, nancummax, nancummin

Each function is invoked on a Vec<Scalar> with a deliberate NaN to
verify the documented "skip nulls by default" behavior matches the
README's claim of pandas-equivalent skipna=True semantics.

Asserts return-type contracts: Float64 for sum/mean/std/var/etc.,
Int64 for count/nunique, Bool for any/all, Option<usize> for argmax/
argmin, Vec<Scalar> with same length for cumulative transforms.

Found via self-review on the last 3 commits — all 23 functions are
re-exported from frankenpandas::prelude (lib.rs:23-27) but had drifted
out of integration coverage.

Verified: 30 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
